summary: Clear soundboard recording that sounds better than remembered, with a solid set highlighted by a beautiful “Friend of the Devil.”
review: The sbd capture is noticeably clearer than fans recalled, delivering good overall fidelity despite some venue‑related quirks. The show itself is regarded as decent – not legendary – but it shines on moments like an especially moving “Friend of the Devil” and a drum section that reminded listeners of a Harley engine roar.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the recording highly (5‑star) and praise its clarity, while the musical performance is described as average to good with a standout song; weighting show (70%) and recording (30%) yields an overall rating around 3.8.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners note the audio sounds much better than their memory, indicating clear mix; no major drop‑outs mentioned but venue and weather caused some technical “demons.”

show_quality.setlist_flow: Overall an okay show with solid camaraderie; the ski‑resort setting made for a quirky atmosphere and the drums segment was especially memorable, though weather and venue issues kept it from being stellar.

band_member_comments.Drums: Drum sound likened to a Harley factory by bikers hanging out during the jam.
